title: Allow skipping Windows registry key operations for first Hyper-V `machine init` and for last Hyper-V `machine rm`
body:
This PRs has 3 commits:

- Minor hyperv stubber.go and vsock.go refactoring
- Hyperv machine init/rm: skip admin operations (windows registry) when possible
- Introduction of a new command: `podman system hyperv-prep`

~~It's still a draft because I need to add the documentation for `podman system hyperv-prep` but early feedback is welcome.~~ PR is ready for review


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/72995484-e095-47ae-b012-91eae67e3d1b


#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
- Introduction of a new command: `podman system hyperv-prep` for Windows administrators to prepare the host for running Hyper-V based podman machines.
- Allow skipping Windows registry key creation for first Hyper-V machine init and skipping deletion for last Hyper-V machine rm.
```

@l0rd just looked at the video. Shouldn't we add a flag to customize the number of hvsock entries to create? I'm thinking about volumes. So they can extend the limit of volumes that can be shared by a machine.

> Should we add a message to inform the command must be run as admin? This is what i see right now.

LGTM

Just one thing which is not blocking.
If you run hyperv-prep with n mounts, then you create a vm that needs n+1 mounts, podman asks you elevation to create the missing hvsock entry, which is good.
However, when you remove the machine, you see 
```
podman.exe machine rm -f
time="2026-04-22T11:56:24+02:00" level=error msg="unable to remove registry entry for 0000D91D-FACB-11E6-BD58-64006A7986D3: \"Access is denied.\""
time="2026-04-22T11:56:24+02:00" level=error msg="unable to remove hvsock registry entries: \"failed to remove sock 0000D91D-FACB-11E6-BD58-64006A7986D3: Access is denied.\""
```

@lstocchi this should be fixed now. I have also updated `podman machine hyperv-prep` to add the user to the Hyper-V admins group. Please take a look.
@containers/podman-maintainers, this is ready for review and is the last in the "Hyper-V-based machines as non-admin" series. @lstocchi has already approved, we need one more to merge. PTAL

> I checked the code and found a few minor issues. I was also thinking it would be good to add some e2e tests, but I'm not sure if that's possible.

Thank you for the review @Honny1. I will make the updates tomorrow.

Adding e2e tests to verify that we don't need administrator privileges to init the first Hyper-V machine isn't possible because CI tasks are run as an administrator. But I have added some unit tests, so that should be fine.

Adding e2e tests for the new `podman system hyperv-prep` command makes more sense. I need to determine where to add that, since it doesn't belong in the `machine e2e` tests (it doesn't involve any machine creation/deletion).
